posts.content_text:
Il Resort in Albania è una copertura per controllare l'Italia dalla Puglia.

Albania migliaia di cittadini stanno protestando contro un grande progetto turistico di lusso sostenuto da Jared Kushner e appoggiato dal governo del premier Edi Rama. Le contestazioni riguardano soprattutto le aree naturali di Rjoll, della Laguna di Narta e dell'Isola di Sazan, considerate tra gli ecosistemi più preziosi del Paese. Ambientalisti, residenti e associazioni denunciano il rischio di danni irreversibili alla biodiversità, alla costa e alle zone protette, accusando il governo di scarsa trasparenza. Le manifestazioni, note anche come "Rivoluzione dei Fenicotteri", chiedono la sospensione dei progetti e una maggiore tutela del patrimonio naturale albanese.  il movimento usa il fenicottero come simbolo perché la zona di Narta ospita una delle più importanti popolazioni di fenicotteri dei Balcani.
L'Isola di Sazan, Narta , Rjoll  appartiene allo Stato Albanese . 
Molti cittadini accusano Edi Rama di aver consegnato proprietà pubbliche e aree di grande valore naturale a investitori stranieri, tra cui Ivanka Trump e Jared Kushner. 
Comunque il progetto è stato sospeso

Il popolo italiano invece dorme!

On June 10, 2026, the biggest Albania protest yet filled central Tirana. The viral shorthand needs one correction.

A Threads post put it this way: “Absolutely massive anti-government protests continue in Albania due to government corruption, democratic backsliding, and the Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump hostile takeover of Sazan island.”

The anger is real. The phrase “hostile takeover” is a slogan, not a verified description.

Reuters reported that thousands marched outside Prime Minister Edi Rama’s office, with the crowd stretching half a mile down a main boulevard. Protesters carried signs reading “Albania is not for sale.” Their target is a Kushner-linked luxury development plan near the Vjosa-Narta protected wetland, plus a separate plan for nearby Sazan Island. Rama told Reuters the projects could be worth up to €5 billion together.

That part matters.

The wetland is home to flamingoes, seals and sea turtle nesting sites. AP reported that a formal environmental impact assessment had not started even though land clearing was already underway inside a nature reserve. Albania’s anti-corruption agency has opened an inquiry related to the project. Rival claims over privatized land remain unresolved.

The demonstrations have grown beyond one resort. Night after night. Reuters reported broader anger over corruption and basic public services after 13 years of Rama’s government. Some protesters are now calling for his resignation. The flamingo has become the movement’s symbol.

There is a useful Canadian lesson here. Governments love the language of jobs, investment and “strategic” projects. Fine. Show the permits. Publish the assessments. Explain who owns the land. Let people see the deal before the bulldozers arrive.

Say it plainly. A luxury development does not get a democratic discount because the investors are rich, connected or arriving by yacht.

#cdnpoli #TheHeadlineLab #Albania #Democracy #Environment

"I am for a new Albania where the moment you get sick, you don't have to open a GoFundMe or go into debt."

Thousands of protesters took to the streets of Tirana for the twelfth straight day on Thursday, rallying outside the Albanian Prime Minister's office in opposition to Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner’s $5 billion luxury resort development project. 

What began as an environmental protection campaign in response to the Trumps’ plan to build near Albania’s Vjosa-Narta protected area and on Sazan Island in the Mediterranean, has grown into a broader movement against corruption in Prime Minister Edi Rama’s government and for housing affordability and public services.

A luxury resort development planned by Donald Trump's daugher Ivanka and her husband Jared Kushner has sparked an uprising against the Albanian government and its prime minister, Edi Rama, who has been in office for 13 years.

For more than 10 days, thousands of people have taken to the streets in Albania's capital, Tirana, seeking initially to halt the construction of a multi-billion-dollar ultra-luxury development on the island of Sazan and the protected coastal wetland Vjosa-Narta — home to endangered species and 1 per cent of the world's flamingo population.

Now that development has been paused, people are continuing to protest against the government, accusing it and Rama of corruption, and demanding he resign.

Read more: https://bit.ly/4ofMW9B

Thousands of Albanians have taken to the streets of Tirana to protest a $5.8 billion luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump. Demonstrators chanted 'Albania Is Not for Sale' and demanded the resignation of Prime Minister Edi Rama, accusing the government of ignoring environmental concerns and public transparency. The controversial project is planned near a protected wetland that is home to flamingos, seals, and sea turtles. As the so-called 'Flamingo Revolution' grows, the European Union is also watching closely, raising questions about Albania's environmental commitments and its future EU membership ambitions. Joshua Barnes tells you more.
#FirstpostLIVE

Si allarga la mobilitazione in Albania contro il progetto turistico di Jared Kushner e Ivanka Trump. Per il quinto giorno consecutivo cittadini, attivisti e organizzazioni ambientaliste sono scesi in piazza per contestare un piano che, secondo i promotori delle proteste, rischierebbe di trasformare una delle aree naturalistiche più delicate del Paese in una destinazione per il turismo di lusso. Al centro della contestazione ci sono l'isola di Sazan, la più grande dell'Albania e finora rimasta ai margini dello sviluppo turistico della costa adriatica, e parte dell'area protetta di Vjosa-Narta, habitat di numerose specie animali tra cui fenicotteri, tartarughe marine e, secondo gli ambientalisti, uno degli ultimi rifugi della foca monaca nel Mediterraneo.

Le manifestazioni, organizzate tra Tirana e la zona di Zvernec, hanno adottato lo slogan "L'Albania non è in vendita". Sui social e nei cortei si è diffusa anche l'espressione "Rivoluzione dei fenicotteri", un riferimento simbolico agli uccelli che popolano la laguna e che i manifestanti considerano minacciati dall'espansione edilizia prevista dal progetto. A rafforzare la mobilitazione hanno contribuito le immagini circolate negli ultimi giorni online. Video che mostrano bulldozer, recinzioni e lavori preparatori lungo la costa hanno alimentato i timori di chi ritiene che il progetto sia già in una fase avanzata. In alcuni casi si sono verificati scontri tra residenti e addetti alla sicurezza privata. Le autorità hanno successivamente revocato le licenze a due società di vigilanza, mentre una guardia è stata arrestata nell'ambito delle indagini sugli incidenti

Migliaia di manifestanti si sono radunati annche oggi a Tirana davanti agli uffici del premier Edi Rama per protestare contro la costruzione di un resort di lusso da 1,6 miliardi di dollari. Il progetto, sostenuto dal fondo Affinity Partners di Jared Kushner e Ivanka Trump, sorgerà in un'area naturale protetta e nell'ex isola militare di Sazan. Gli attivisti, scesi in piazza con fenicotteri gonfiabili nella cosiddetta "Flamingo Revolution", denunciano la distruzione dell'ecosistema locale e la mancanza di trasparenza nei permessi. Le proteste — che durano ormai da più di una settimana — sono nate a seguito della comparsa delle prime ruspe e recinzioni nell'area costiera e della diffusione virale di un video in cui un attivista veniva trascinato via con la forza dalla sicurezza privata.

300,000 Albanian people came together to stop a nature reserve from being turned into a millionaire's playground. A beautiful example of the power of a united people fighting for the greater good. ❤️🇦🇱

Repost @breakingtrendsnews 
——
Hundreds of protesters gathered in Albania to oppose a proposed luxury resort project on Sazan Island, reportedly backed by businessman Jared Kushner.
.
Demonstrators raised concerns about environmental protection, public access, and the future of one of the country's most unique coastal areas.

#Albania #SazanIsland #Protest #jaredkushner

Ganja Granny sez The protest started before 2pm and ended after three, proceeding down the avenue. I'm with the Albanians.... I don't have to go anywhere. The protest is downstairs from where I live... ALBANIA is not for sale....Albanians and local activists are gathering in front of the Consulate General of Albania on East 79th Street (between 1st and 2nd Avenue) in Manhattan at 2:00 PM.The demonstration is part of a wave of international solidarity rallies in major cities. Protesters are speaking out against the controversial $1.6 billion luxury resort and tourism projects proposed for protected environmental and coastal areas in Albania (such as Sazan Island and the Vjosa-Narta wetland), which are linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka

For the past week, citizens of Albania have been rallying in the streets to protest the controversial Trump-Kushner resort project. Ivanka Trump and her husband, Jared Kushner, plan to build a luxury resort on the uninhabited Sazan Island. The protected land is part of the Karaburun-Sazan Marine National Park, Albania’s first marine protected area. Additional developments are also set to be built on an undeveloped stretch of coastline near the Vjosa-Narta Protected Landscape. This highly sensitive wetland ecosystem serves as a vital sanctuary for flamingos, pelicans, monk seals and sea turtle nesting sites.

Residents and activists in Albania continue to oppose a controversial resort development linked to Jared Kushner on and around Sazan Island and nearby protected coastal areas.

Critics of the project argue that the development could restrict public access, affect sensitive ecosystems, and alter areas regarded as part of Albania’s natural and cultural heritage. Environmental groups and local residents have organized demonstrations and campaigns calling for greater transparency and stronger protections for the region.

#Albania #SazanIsland #JaredKushner #Environment #Protest

Albania just gave its protest movement a name — the Flamingo Revolution.
A $1.4 billion resort linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump is planned on Sazan Island and the nearby Vjosa delta — home to around 3,000 flamingos, sea turtles and Dalmatian pelicans.  (MS NOW)
Bulldozers have already entered the area. Environmental groups confirmed at least one sea turtle nest has already been destroyed.  (CBS News)
Anti-corruption prosecutors have opened a formal investigation into how the land was acquired and sold to the investors.  (The Washington Post)
Ivanka said she discovered the island from a friend's boat. Albanians say their coastline was never lost.
The people are in the streets. The flamingos are on the signs. And the bulldozers are still running.
Whose side are you on? Drop it below.

#FlamingoRevolution #Albania #JaredKushner #IvankaTrump #Trump #usa #tirana #EnvironmentNews #WorldNews #fyp #foryoupage

Ivanka Trump in Albania: Why Protesters Are Demanding She Go Home

Thousands of protesters have taken to the streets of Albania with a clear message: "Ivanka, go home!" In this video, we dive deep into the growing controversy surrounding two massive luxury resort projects linked to Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner.

Valued at over $5 billion, these developments through their investment firm have ignited a firestorm of environmental concerns and political unrest. From protected wetlands on the Adriatic coast to the transformation of Sazan Island (a former military zone), we explore why locals are clashing with security guards and demanding political change.

Are these projects a golden ticket for Albania’s economy, or a threat to its most precious ecosystems? We also cover the latest anti-corruption investigations into land ownership transfers that have added fuel to the fire.

In this video, you will learn about:

The specific locations of the Trump-Kushner projects in Albania.
Why environmentalists are sounding the alarm over the Adriatic coast.
The "Ivanka Go Home" movement and its political demands.
The $5 billion economic impact vs. the cost of natural destruction.
Ongoing investigations by anti-corruption prosecutors.

#IvankaTrump #Albania #JaredKushner #Protest #Environment #GlobalNews #LuxuryRealEstate #BreakingNews #SazanIsland #PoliticalUnrest

We extend our solidarity to the people of Albania and the environmental groups there who are protesting and fighting against the ruin of their natural heritage at the altar of capitalism. 

🇦🇱 We believe in the power of protest, and we stand with those who are taking to the streets, who are writing about the cause, and who are battling in court for social and environmental justices.

Vjosa-Narta is a protected biodiverse lagoon at the mouth of one of the last wild rivers of Europe, overlooking what should be the nature reserve of Sazan Island. 

⚠️ But it is under the imminent threat of being swallowed up by a sprawling luxury resort, planned by none other than Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner with the collaboration of the Albanian government. 

An activist from Moviment Graffitti was on site this spring with one of the local resistance and nature conservation organisations, the AOS-Albanian Ornithological Society, and experienced first-hand the area's natural splendour. 

🇲🇹 The heartache from knowing such a special place is threatened by commercialisation is a familiar feeling to us here. 

Public land should not be sold to commercial interests. Nature is not a backdrop for private profits. Protected areas are not a luxury commodity.

Protect Albania's Protected Areas!

Protect the Vjosa Delta!

"Albania is not for sale!" Watch emotional crowds march through Zvërnec to protest Jared Kushner's planned €1.4B luxury resort, which threatens a pristine wetland home to 7,000 flamingos. Official Reuters footage from Saturday, June 6, 2026, capturing the intense public backlash along the Adriatic coast in Zvërnec, Albania. Thousands of local citizens and ecologists have taken to the streets, holding Albanian flags and inflatable flamingos to block a massive luxury resort project pushed by Jared Kushner's firm, Affinity Partners. We examine the major environmental threat to the Vjosa-Narta protected wetland, the sudden arrival of barbed-wire fences on public beaches, and the expanding anti-corruption investigation (SPAK) into the legal reforms that authorized the mega-resort.

{AC1C}

Kushner Albania resort protest 2026, Zvernec luxury development Affinity Partners, Vjosa Narta protected landscape flamingos, Edi Rama SPAK investigation land corruption, Sazan island development Trump son in law, Ivanka Trump go home protest Tirana, euronews Albania environment rally, Mediterranean monk seal habitat threat, beach access barbed wire clash, June 6 news pool Reuters

#AlbaniaProtest #JaredKushner #Zvernec #VjosaNarta #Flamingos #AffinityPartners #EdiRama #SaveNarta #EcoActivism #BreakingNews2026 #June6

Albanians protest for an eleventh straight night against the Kushner-Trump family backed resort project on Sazan Island.

Demonstrators are demanding the government cancel the development of a luxury resort planned by Jared Kushner's Affinity Partners and Ivanka Trump on a former communist military island and the protected Vjosa-Narta coastal wetland.

"Albania is not for sale!"🔗👇https://www.aa.com.tr/en/europe/protests-against-tourism-project-in-albania-continue-unabated/3963555

Albania is protesting a $1.4 billion luxury resort planned by Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner on Sazan Island, a former military island in southern Albania's Narta Lagoon. The project covers the island plus nearby coastline and has sparked days of demonstrations with 8,000–10,000 people in Tirana. 

Protesters fear environmental damage to the protected wildlife sanctuary home to 200+ bird species and Mediterranean monk seals, while also raising concerns about corruption and illegal land privatization.

Prime Minister Edi Rama supports the resort, claiming it will boost tourism and aid EU membership, and his government changed laws to approve 5-star developments. 

However, Albania's anti-corruption agency is investigating the project. Ivanka Trump angered locals by calling Sazan a "private island that we had uncovered," leading Albanians to nickname it "Island of the Trumps." 

Protesters use pink flamingo symbols since flamingos are protected migratory birds, and this marks the second Trump-family Balkan project facing opposition after a Belgrade hotel was cancelled last year.

posts.author_handle: The TRUST FALL: Julian Assange - Documentary
posts.content_text:
What happened in Albania this week was a massive, hard-fought win for the people, proving that relentless public pressure and unwavering awareness are the only true forces capable of dismantling elite greed.

It is truly a remarkable coincidence that Jared Kushner just happened to randomly pick a highly strategic former submarine base for a "luxury eco-resort."

The development of luxury resorts on Albania’s Sazan Island and the Zvërnec Peninsula were painted by the elite as a “tourism and investment opportunity” - but are quite obviously planned to serve a strategic purpose for Israel and the U.S. - due to their military history and highly sensitive geographical position. Essentially, an outpost for building "Greater Israel". It is a classic imperial bait-and-switch.

To grasp the sheer scale of the rot, we must understand that plutocracy and necrocapitalism are deeply related, as a plutocratic government creates the exact political conditions that allow necrocapitalism to exist and profit. While plutocracy describes who holds the power (the ultra-wealthy), necrocapitalism describes a method of making money-specifically, a form of capitalism where profit is derived from death, destruction, subjugation, and the exploitation of vulnerable human lives. This grim reality is the beating heart of our current imperial world order.

The movement ignited on May 23, 2026, when residents and activists in Zvërnec mobilized against illegal fencing blocking public access to the Vjosa–Narta Protected Landscape. This spontaneous resistance rapidly expanded as demonstrators utilized social media and public rallies to confront state-backed projects and assert their right to communal spaces.

The sheer scale of the grift is breathtaking to behold. According to Reuters, the project on the Zvërnec peninsula was slated to cost $1.6 billion and build 10,000 rooms, directly threatening the Vjosa-Narta protected wetlands. They want to just rock up and bulldoze a literal paradise just to lay down some concrete for the global elite's next playground and a military base underneath it.

The imperial war machine looks at a fragile, breathing ecosystem and sees only a blank canvas for its own expansion. BirdLife International reports that the Vjosa-Narta Protected Landscape in Albania, targeted by U.S. developers, is a critical habitat for over 200 bird species and endangered marine life, including the Mediterranean monk seal and the area's iconic pink flamingos.

And I’m sure the 3,600 nuclear-hardened military bunkers on Sazan Island would make fabulous wine cellars for wealthy tourists, and certainly wouldnt' be secretly repurposed as a forward operating base for Western intelligence agencies.

We should definitely trust that the U.S. empire and its Israeli proxy-who have spent the last year turning Gaza into a graveyard-are suddenly deeply passionate about providing high-end Mediterranean ecotourism to the Albanian people. This sickening pitch is the kind of brazen lie that only a heavily insulated political class could peddle with a straight face to the working class masses.

The reality of their investments is infinitely more violent. The Council on Foreign Relations documented that since October 2023, the U.S. has supplied Israel with over $12.5 billion in military aid, directly funding the wholesale slaughter of Palestinian civilians and the destruction of Gaza. That is precisely what their blood money actually builds in the global south.

But people globally are finally waking up to the interconnected nature of this menace. The fierce resistance shown by Albanian protestors is inextricably linked to the anti-colonial struggle of the Palestinian people, as both are fighting against a savage Western imperialism that thrives on displacement, environmental destruction, and death. We are all bound together in this shared global struggle.

The local outrage is completely justified. Marxist.com noted that over 800,000 Albanians have been driven out of their country by the economic ravages of capitalism since the 1990s, yet thousands returned to the streets to protest the Kushner resort in what has been dubbed the "Flamingo Revolution". People are finally getting fed up with this systemic capitalist rot.

The people on the ground clearly understand the dark gravity of what is unfolding. The Albanian populace has awakened to the parasitic synergy between global oligarchs and the systematic erosion of their borders. They have forged a desperate, unified front to repel this encroaching shadow, standing like a rusted iron bulkhead slamming shut against a rising tide of black oil, a grim testament to a nation clawing back its autonomy from those who would see it liquidated.

This people-powered pushback has actually managed to throw a massive spanner in the works. Legally, public pressure forced the Albanian government to suspend construction pending an Environmental Impact Assessment, while the anti-corruption body (SPAK) probes the project's privatisation and legislative changes removing protected status. Politically, the outcome remains deadlocked; despite united citizen protests, Prime Minister Edi Rama remains defiant, declaring the investment will proceed as long as he is in office.

Despite the sheer arrogance of their leaders, this grassroots momentum is a beacon of hope. The "Flamingo Revolution" in Albania is an undeniable victory of the working people over necrocapitalism, proving that grassroots movements can successfully defeat the parasitic forces that seek to commodify and militarise their homeland. We must learn from their courage today.

Every delayed bulldozer is a testament to the sheer power of human solidarity. The victory over the developers at the Zvërnec Peninsula proves that when ordinary citizens unite against the rogue state machinations of Washington and Tel Aviv, the ruthless, blood-soaked engine of necrocapitalism can stopped in its tracks.

The fight is far from over, but the path forward has never been clearer or more fiercely urgent for us all. Francesca Albanese posted this week on X: “Necrocapitalism seems invincible - but it is not! All we need is unity, coordination, perseverance. Keep Protesting. Striking. Boycotting. Litigating. Not just once. All the times needed till the Apartheid ends and the system which fed it too.” We must forcefully dismantle the war machine and reclaim our shared humanity before there is nothing left. This is the ultimate test of our generation, and we cannot afford to fail here.

We're craving a way to break this engineered coma by chucking a figurative rock at the empire’s propaganda machine. 'THE TRUST FALL' documentary is the perfect antidote to manufactured obedience. It rips away polished media facades, forcing us to witness the moral void of the U.S.-Israeli death cult. It’s a crowbar to pry open closed minds before the West burns everything.

When we share the truth, we light a countless torches in the darkness of imperial deception.

It's available worldwide to watch for free, but crushing these imperial grifters requires everyday punters giving a damn enough to pay it forward and help expand the reach of the film.

Thanks to the financial support of donors, we were able to show THE TRUST FALL for free to 1,203 people in the past week. Let’s keep amplifying our collective voice.

Please support this educational project on gofundme: https://gofund.me/55f992e2 or PayPal: paypal.me/filmsforchange (Films For Change)

🕊 Watch ‘THE TRUST FALL’ film for FREE 👉🏼 www.thetrustfall.org/watch

THE TRUST FALL is more than a film. It is a peace movement.

In solidarity,

Kym Staton

Writer and Director
THE TRUST FALL

Albania Protests Grow Over Trump Family-Linked Resort Project

Crowds have gathered once again on Albania’s eastern coast to protest a planned multi-million dollar resort project linked to an investment firm involving Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner.

Demonstrators say the development threatens the fragile coastal ecosystem and could endanger local wildlife.

Protests have also spread to the capital, where thousands are calling for the resignation of Prime Minister Edi Rama over his support for the project.

The dispute highlights growing tensions between economic development plans and environmental protection in the region..

#aljazeeraenglish #BreakingNews #aljazeeralivenews #aljazeeraenglish   #newsfeed #news #aljazera #latestnews #BreakingNews #news #AlbaniaProtest #usa

Perché in Albania si protesta, la "Rivoluzione dei Fenicotteri" contro il resort del genero di Trump: "Non siamo in vendita"

Chi scende in piazza chiede le dimissioni del premier Edi Rama che accusa "nemici dell'Albania e di Israele". Gli interessi di Washington nei Balcani 

Non si fermano le proteste in Albania innescate dal resort progettato anche da Jared Kushner, genero del Presidente degli Stati Uniti Donald Trump, in un’area costiera protetta. Continuano e si trasformano in un largo movimento di opposizione al governo. Chi scende in piazza, ormai per il decimo giorno consecutivo, chiede le dimissioni del primo ministro Edi Rama, che in realtà ha dichiarato come il progetto non si ancora ufficiale mentre l’interesse di Washington nell’area dei Balcani appare evidente. “L’Albania non è in vendita”, lo slogan ripetuto in piazza dai manifestanti che assicurano che le proteste continueranno.

Soprannominata “Rivoluzione dei Fenicotteri”, l’ondata di mobilitazione è stata innescata dal progetto turistico di un resort di lusso progettato nell’area di Pishe Poro e sull’isola di Sazan. Un piano articolato in due interventi, annunciato inizialmente nel 2024, per un investimento complessivo di oltre 5 miliardi di euro: lo sviluppo costiero nell’area della laguna di Narta, tra le altre cose riserva naturale, e un resort sull’isola disabitata di Sazan, che ospitava una base militare nell’epoca comunista. A investire sarebbe la Atlantic Incubation Partners, società legata alla Affinity Partners, il fondo di investimenti di Kushner. Per il governo un progetto che incentiverebbe il turismo di lusso sulla costa adriatica. Secondo il New York Times, dietro il progetto ci sarebbero dei magnati del Qatar di origini siriane.

Le proteste sono esplose quando un manifestante, il 30 maggio scorso, è stato picchiato dalla sicurezza privata. I leader hanno fatto notare la coincidenza con l’anniversario della fondazione della Lega di Prizren, episodio storico dell’800 considerato un luogo della memoria simbolo dell’unità nazionale dell’Albania. I fenicotteri rosa sono tra le specie protette che popolano le zone del progetto. I manifestanti hanno avanzato delle richieste: le dimissioni del governo, l’abrogazione dello status speciale per gli investitori strategici, il ritiro della normativa chiamata “Pacchetto Montagna”, l’annullamento delle modifiche alla legge sulle aree protette, l’abrogazione delle modifiche alla legge sul patrimonio culturale.

Rama, in carica dal 2013 e al suo quarto mandato da premier, ha respinto le accuse a Euronews descrivendo le proteste come alimentate e amplificate da bot, narrazioni antisemite e forze esterne ostili. Alla CNN ha accusato “nemici dell’Albania e di Israele”. Sullo sfondo aleggia il processo di ingresso nell’Unione Europea, per il quale Tirana è candidata. "L’Albania dovrebbe astenersi da azioni che potrebbero compromettere il raggiungimento del criterio di chiusura, in questo caso il Capitolo 27", ha precisato il portavoce Guillaume Mercier, riferendosi al capitolo dei negoziati di adesione che impone ai Paesi candidati di allinearsi alle norme per la tutela dell'ambiente.

La vicenda racchiude diversi dei temi centrali per il Paese: dalla speculazione edilizia al turismo, dalle privatizzazioni alla difesa del patrimonio. Lo scorso febbraio la vicepremier e ministra delle Infrastrutture Belinda Balluku si ea dimessa per un’indagine sulle procedure d’appalto. Il primo giugno la Procura albanese speciale contro la corruzione e il crimine organizzato (SPAK) ha aperto un fascicolo per indagare i suddetti controversi emendamenti alla legge sulle aree protette. La Affinity Partners aveva comunque avviato la costruzione di un hotel di lusso a Belgrado al posto del Ministero della Difesa distrutto dai bombardamenti NATO del 1999, tutto saltato dopo un’indagine della procura serba.

🚨 ALBANIA IN PIAZZA DA 12 GIORNI: MIGLIAIA DI PERSONE CONTRO IL PROGETTO DA 4 MILIARDI DI DOLLARI LEGATO ALLA FAMIGLIA TRUMP

Continuano senza sosta le proteste in Albania contro il maxi-investimento immobiliare promosso da Affinity Partners, società collegata a Jared Kushner e Ivanka Trump.

Migliaia di cittadini, attivisti e associazioni stanno manifestando a Tirana e nella zona costiera di Zvërnec per chiedere lo stop al progetto, ribattezzando la mobilitazione "Rivoluzione dei Fenicotteri".

Al centro della contestazione c'è un piano da oltre 4 miliardi di dollari che prevede la costruzione di ville e resort di lusso sull'isola di Sazan e nell'area protetta di Vjosa-Narta, uno dei territori naturalistici più importanti del Paese.

Secondo gli ambientalisti, il progetto rischia di compromettere habitat protetti, biodiversità marina e le rotte migratorie dei fenicotteri. I manifestanti accusano inoltre il governo di Edi Rama di aver favorito l'operazione attraverso modifiche legislative che avrebbero agevolato gli investimenti privati in aree sensibili.

Il governo albanese respinge le accuse e sostiene che il progetto porterà crescita economica, nuovi posti di lavoro e un forte sviluppo turistico.

Anche la Commissione Europea ha espresso preoccupazione per il possibile impatto ambientale dell'iniziativa.

Sviluppo economico o tutela dell'ambiente: da che parte state?

AMV NEWS. WED, 10 JUNE. (TEXT VERSION)
​
Back and forth continues in the middle east. Platner wins and Mace is out after primary elections in Maine and South Carolina. Five european countries formally sanction far right Israeli leaders. Albania protests continue. Republicans look to cut healthcare again along with social security. Distrust with Hegseth at the Pentagon increases.​ Musk is sued in mississippi and more. 

STRIKES CONTINUE IN MIDDLE EAST
​U.S. military officials said in a statement Tuesday that it had completed “self-defense strikes” at Trump’s direction in response to the downing of the helicopter, hitting Iranian air defense sites, ground-control stations and surveillance radar​.

Iran’s Foreign Ministry ​then condemned the U.S. attacks in a statement Wednesday and said it had “severely hit US bases and assets in the region that were the source of these aggressions.” The Iranian retaliation included attacks on the U.S. Fifth Fleet based in Bahrain and Ali al-Salem base in Kuwait, according to Iranian state media reports. The U.S. has not confirmed the attacks, but Kuwait’s military reported intercepting aerial attacks, and Jordan’s armed forces said they intercepted five missiles from Iran. No deaths were reported​. (Source: The Hill)

P​LATNER WINS AND MACE OUT AFTER PRIMARIES
Graham Platner won the Maine Democratic primary for Senate after weathering allegations about his past, formally setting up the November bout for a seat that could be pivotal in determining which party controls the Senate.​ (Source: Washington Post) 

Rep. Josh Gottheimer (D-N.J.) on Tuesday​ before the primary said Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner should win his primary on Tuesday and then get off the ballot so that Democrats can replace him with a stronger candidate in the fall.

Gottheimer has received over 2 million dollars from pro-israeli pacs (Source: The Hill)

﻿
﻿​Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) endorsed South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson (R) in the Palmetto State’s GOP gubernatorial primary after she lost the initial primary Tuesday.

​Wilson goes up against Republican Lt Gov Pamela Evette, who received President Trump’s endorsement​ in a June 23 runoff for South Carolina governor after no candidate won more than half of the vote outright in the GOP primary.  

Mace came in fifth after pushing for the release of the Epstein files and drawing the ire of Trump. (Source: The Hill) 

Nancy Lacore, a three-star admiral that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth fired last year, is projected head to a runoff against veteran Mac Deford in the Democratic primary for South Carolina’s 1st Congressional District, ​Nancy Maces seat in Congress. 

Dr Annie Andrews and Lindsey Graham will face off in November for his US Senate Seat. 

﻿​60 MINUTES ANCHOR DESCRIBES MANIPULATION

​The fired 60 Minutes anchor Scott Pelley has accused editorial management at CBS of interfering with a broadcast segment on the killing of the Minneapolis protester Renee Good by an immigration officer in January.
The veteran broadcaster, who was recently dismissed from the show, said CBS News’s editor-in-chief, Bari Weiss, had sent an email to his supervisor requesting changes shortly before the airing of the segment in question.

​Pelley told the outlet: “Two of the things in the email include, ‘Can we make the protesters look more violent?’ Now, I’m paraphrasing. I don’t have the quote, but that’s what was communicated to me. And the other thing, Renee Good’s car. You need to describe her as driving toward the officer.”​ (Source: The Guardian) 

I​SRAEL FAR RIGHT LEADERS SANCTIONED

Australia, Canada, New Zealand, Norway and the United Kingdom will formally sanction far-right Israeli ministers, Itamar Ben-Gvir and Bezalel Smotrich, for “incitement of violence” against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and Gaza, their foreign ministers said in a joint statement.

Israel’s national security minister, Ben-Gvir, who has previously been convicted in Israel of incitement to racism and supporting a “terror” organisation, and Minister of Finance Smotrich will now face freezes on their assets and travel bans from the five countries following the announcement on Tuesday​. (Source: The Guardian) 

​EUROPEAN CONFIDENCE IN US AT HISTORIC LOW

European confidence in an American “security guarantee” has hit a historic low, a survey suggests, with only one in 10 people across 15 countries seeing the US as an ally and majorities in all doubting it would come to their aid if they were attacked.

The survey, published on Wednesday by the European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) thinktank before critical G7 and Nato summits in France and Turkey over the coming weeks, revealed “deep European distrust in the US”, the authors said.​ (Source: The Guardian) 

M​USK SUED BY MISSISSIPPI
Elon Musk’s xAI and SpaceX have been sued by Mississippi residents who say a power plant fueling nearby data centers is blasting “omnipresent ​and inescapable” noise that has eroded their health and home ‌values.
The lawsuit,made public on Tuesday in federal court in Oxford, Mississippi, claims Musk’s companies negligently failed to curb the disturbance and created a public nuisance through excessive ​and offensive noise. Three residents filed the case on behalf of ​a class estimated at more than 10,000 members.​ (Source: Reuters) 

G​OP COMING AFTER HEALTHCARE AND SOCIAL SECURITY

In a ​r​ecent interview ​Republican House Speaker Mik Johnson said: "The largest spending items, the reason we're in trouble is because over 74 percent of federal spending is on autopilot—mandatory spending, that is your entitlement programs like Medicare, Medicaid, and things like Social Security—they have to be adjusted and fixed.

"We have a plan to do that next year, and it's critical, because we're at $40 trillion plus in debt. At some point you get into a hole so deep you can't climb out of it, so desperate times call for desperate measures."

​Johnson took part in the previous two "beautiful bills" under Trump administrations which gave almost 7 trillion of dollars in tax cuts to wealthy billionaires and corporations while now pushing for an extra half a trillion more in defense spending. (Source: Newsweek) 

​NY LAWMAKERS PASS AI PAUSE
On the final day of the state's legislative session on June 4, New York lawmakers passed a bill that would pause permits for new data center construction in the state for one year, although Governor Kathy Hochul has indicated she may not be in favor of such a move.

The governor has until December 31 to approve the bill now that the legislative session has ended. If she does not sign it by this date, the bill automatically does not become law. Hochul received the bill June 4 and can sign or veto it at any time in that window.​ (Source: NY Times) 

DELANEY UPDATE
There are reports that the Delaney hunger strike is now over as strikers have lost the ability to continue following two weeks of protests over conditions inside the facility. (Source: On the ground reporters)

​ALBANIA PROTESTS CONTINUE

Albania will plough on with a luxury resort planned by Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner on a remote stretch of Balkan coast despite persistent protests over its environmental impact, Prime Minister Edi Rama told Reuters on Monday.
Thousands of people have ‌taken to the streets of the capital Tirana and on the southern coast where the resort has been proposed, calling for the project to be scrapped because of the impact on a protected wetland home to flamingoes, seals and sea turtle nesting sites but also venting a litany of grievances against Rama’s 13-year tenure that they say has failed to uproot corruption or improve basic services.
“We are getting bigger and we are here until he resigns. Not only for biodiversity but for every injustice we face,” said student Albano Lushi.
The resort development Rama is championing is the brainchild of Kushner and his wife, Ivanka Trump, who described falling in love with Albania a few years ago while visiting on a boat. Rama met them on that trip and found them to be "very nice, humble...humanly good people."
Now, Kushner's investment firm Affinity Partners is ⁠involved in the €1.4 billion ($1.6 billion) project near the Vjosa-Narta protected area, and another one on nearby Sazan Island.
Together the projects are worth up to €5 billion, Rama said.
Albania's state anti-corruption agency has confirmed it opened an investigation related to the project but has not disclosed details.

However, the demise of a similar project in Serbia offers a cautionary tale. In November, Serbia's Parliament passed a special law to enable the building of a luxury complex in the capital, Belgrade, to be financed by an investment company linked to Kushner.

The following month, Serbia's prosecutor for organized crime charged four people, including a government minister, with abuse of office and falsifying of documents to help pave the way for the development. (Source: The independent) 

HEGSETH CRIPPLING PENTAGON

Secrecy, paranoia and distrust are running rampant inside Pete Hegseth’s Pentagon, crippling decision-making and preparedness, according to a report.

The former Fox News host has fired more than two dozen senior officers, pushed out a Navy secretary and personally intervened in promotions across all four military branches, according to CNN.

Fifteen current and former Pentagon officials have revealed the current culture of distrust and paranoia that has plagued the Department of Defense ever since Hegseth took over, telling CNN that troops have to sign nondisclosure agreements and submit to polygraph tests to learn about operations. (Source: The Independent)

Conduzco a los Balcanes a menudo. Trabajo y privado y me gusta mucho. Por eso sigo de cerca los acontecimientos en Albania. Estudié un montón de material y escribí un comentario que ofrece una perspectiva ligeramente diferente.
Es necesario leer todo el artículo, pero intento señalar que la isla de Sazán es una ubicación estratégica del mar Adriático. Y Europa lo es. Solía haber una importante base militar allí. Ahora un complejo de lujo conectado a Jared Kushner e Ivanka Trump se supone que crece allí. Pero el dinero a la compañía de Kushner fluye de las ricas monarquías sunitas del Golfo Pérsico - Arabia Saudita, Qatar y los Emiratos Árabes Unidos. Y es por eso que surge la pregunta de si Albania, un país miembro de la OTAN, no es mucho más que turismo, playas, flamencos y tortugas.
Podría ser la seguridad de toda Europa. Quién controla a Sazan, controla parte del Adriático.
Albania está hablando de desarrollo turístico. Europa hasta ahora es principalmente ecología. Edi Rama sobre la modernización del país. Jared Kushner en un complejo de lujo. Ivanka Trumpova sobre el romántico "descubrimiento" de la isla virgen.
Pero tal vez todos estamos mirando hacia el lado equivocado.
Sazan no es solo una isla hermosa. Es un punto estratégico a la entrada al golfo de Vlora, en el espacio del estrecho de Otranta, es decir, la puerta marítima al mar Adriático. Es un lugar entre Albania e Italia, supervisado por la Unión Europea, en la frontera de los mares de Jader y Jónico, rutas cercanas que son importantes para el comercio, migración, energía, logística marítima y seguridad.
Por lo tanto, no se trata sólo de turismo y ecología, aunque la naturaleza será la primera víctima. También es una pregunta de seguridad. ¿A quién y por qué vendemos lugares estratégicos en Europa?
Toda la UE debería estar alerta y no sólo hablar de biodiversidad.
Los preparativos ya están en proceso y no sería posible sin la cooperación de las autoridades albanesas, y ya en 2024 comenzaron a cambiar las leyes para hacer posible el proyecto.

Ivanka Trump and Her Husband Kushner Turned Albania Upside Down

• Albania Is Not for Sale.. Ongoing Protests Against Their Luxury Tourism Project

For the sixth consecutive day, Albanians are taking to the streets in large-scale protests under the slogan “Albania Is Not for Sale,” rejecting a massive luxury tourism resort project backed by Jared Kushner and his wife Ivanka Trump.

The project, valued between $1.4 and $5 billion, extends across Sazan Island (a former military base) and large parts of the protected Vjosa-Narta wetlands near Vlora. Albanians fear that the construction of hotels, villas, and a marina will destroy this ecologically sensitive area, which is home to flamingos, sea turtles, Mediterranean seals, and hundreds of species of migratory birds.

Despite the widespread public anger, Prime Minister Edi Rama strongly defends the project, describing it as a major tourism investment that will create jobs. Meanwhile, Albania’s anti-corruption authority has launched an official investigation into the land allocation process and the legal changes made to facilitate the project.

The protests continue amid a growing sentiment that Albania is being sold to major foreign interests.

Yes, Albanian authorities have launched a formal anti-corruption probe into a $4 billion luxury resort project linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, bringing the development to a halt amid frozen developer assets and mass public protests.

The sweeping probe has heavily impacted the development with several key details:

The Investigation: 
Albania's Special Prosecution Office Against Corruption and Organized Crime (SPAK) opened a formal inquiry into how land titles and ownership were acquired. 

The probe specifically targets changes made to the legal and protected status of the land in 2024, which bypassed normal tenders and paved the way for foreign tourism development.

Frozen Assets: 
Following the opening of the corruption probe, authorities froze the bank accounts and assets of landholding companies connected to the Kushner-backed development.

The Project: 
Backed by Kushner's firm Affinity Partners, the proposal involves building luxury hotels and a 10,000-room complex across Sazan Island and the surrounding protected Vjosa-Narta coastal landscape.

Public Backlash: 
The plans sparked days of massive, ongoing protests in the capital city of Tirana. Demonstrators, who dubbed the movement the "Flamingo Revolution," are outraged over the development's environmental impact on sensitive wetlands and sea turtle nesting sites.

Government Stance: 
Despite the assets being frozen and the ongoing corruption probe, Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama has continued to defend the investment, insisting it is necessary for the country's economic future.

Reason for Protest: Jared Kushner is getting Albania’s largest island on a 99-year lease — zero land cost, zero investment from his own pocket.

The Deal is not finalised but almost near.

The island already has 3,600 bunkers, a naval port and roads built by Albanian taxpayers during decades of Cold War military use. Kushner didn’t build any of it. He’s just getting it.

Who actually pays? Gulf sovereign funds — Saudi, UAE and Qatar — cover the $4 billion construction cost. Kushner manages the money and collects the profits.

What did Albania get in return? They rewrote their own laws, stripped the island’s protected status, built an airport and blocked their own citizens from their beaches.

All for Trump’s goodwill.

An Albanian whose grandfather served on that island now needs a $3,000 per night resort booking just to set foot on it.

Thousands are protesting. Anti-corruption prosecutors opened an investigation. PM Rama says the deal is not stopping.

Sazan Island is nearly 20x bigger than Epstein’s island. And it just got handed away for 99 years.
